dominant_hooks: "After years of knee pain, I thought my running days were over. HF Cloud barefoot shoes reignited my passion!" — pain-to-transformation runner testimonial (oldest running ads Dec 2025, highest impression proxy)
"I've never stepped into a shoe this easily—no bending, no fumbling. HF Stride's Hands-Off Entry is a game-changer!" – Maria, 58 — step-in convenience testimonial (8+ top-50 ads)

"She won't say anything. That's just who she is. She'll wince getting up from the couch and tell you she's fine. She'll slow down on walks and say she's just tired." — caregiver/gift-buyer emotional hook
"Why Your Balance Got Worse Over Time" — educational mechanism: "Your feet have thousands of nerve endings that send constant signals to your brain... Thick, cushioned soles muffle those signals."
"Afraid of falling? You're not alone 😰 HF Stride barefoot shoes are designed to keep you steady and safe" — direct fear-of-falling address (4+ ads in top 50)
"1 in 4 adults over 60 will fall this year. Thousands will suffer life-changing injuries 😨 Don't become a statistic ❌" — statistics-based fear trigger
"Balance issues and falls aren't inevitable. They're preventable. And it often comes down to one thing. What's on your feet." — reframe/solution bridge
"The burning keeps coming back because the compression never stops. Your shoe is squeezing the exact nerves causing it every single hour you wear it." — neuropathy mechanism indictment hook

"Most barefoot shoes work great. And look terrible. So women skip them. And miss out on everything barefoot footwear could do for their balance, their feet, and their future. Flame just ended that trade-off completely." — aesthetics vs. function reframe
"We're so confident these shoes will improve your balance that we'll let you test them for 30 days. If they don't work, you don't pay. Full refund. No questions." — guarantee-forward confidence hook
avatar_profiles: Senior woman 60-70 with chronic foot/knee pain (plantar fasciitis, neuropathy) who was previously active (runner, walker) but has had to slow down — targeted by runner-to-recovery testimonial and cloud comfort messaging
Senior 65-75 with neuropathy or diabetic foot — experiences burning, tingling, stabbing sensations; targeted by 'compression never stops' and 'stepping on broken glass' copy
Senior 65+ with balance and fall concerns — has slowed down, grips furniture, fears falling; targeted by fear-of-falling stats and steady/confident steps messaging

Senior man 65-75 with mobility limitations — can't bend down easily to put on shoes, wants independence; targeted by Hands-Off Entry angle ('I'm 68, and balance has been my biggest challenge... slip them on completely hands-free')
Adult daughter 40-55 buying for aging mom who 'won't say anything' — highly emotional caregiver gift-buyer angle ('She Says She's Fine. She's Not.')
Style-conscious senior woman who has rejected medical-looking shoes — targeted by Flame product and 'Most barefoot shoes work great. And look terrible.' copy
pain_points: Foot pain / nerve pain / neuropathy — burning, tingling, stabbing, sensitivity (referenced in 20+ of 50 ads via testimonials and direct copy)
Balance problems / fear of falling — loss of stability, wobbling, fear of injury (keyword 'balance' in 25/50 ads)
Cramped/squeezed toes in narrow traditional shoes — wide toe box solution mentioned in 24/50 ads
Inability to bend down to put on shoes — mobility limitation (hands-off/step-in angle in 16/50 ads)
Raised heels / unnatural posture from footwear — zero-drop solution in 15/50 ads
Slowing down / losing independence / not walking as far as before (8+ ads reference this emotional consequence)
Fear of fall-related injuries — life-altering statistics hook
Clunky ugly medical-looking shoes that signal disability — aesthetic rejection angle in newer Dataset A ads
offer_mechanics: 50% OFF (most common discount — 'HUGE Sale', 'Final Hours: Save 50%', 'Top models 50% off')
36% OFF (evergreen standard discount in guarantee-forward ads)
30-Day Money-Back Guarantee (nearly every ad — sometimes framed as 'Prevent Falls Or Don't Pay' headline)
Scarcity stacks: 'Only 100 Pairs Left', '99 Pairs Left', '3 Days Left', 'FINAL HOURS', 'LAST REMAINING STOCK'
Clearance price: $33/pair, 3 for $99 (one-time blitz Feb 2026 for discontinued colorways)
$69.96 entry price limited launch (new Flame model)

ad_examples.headline: She Says She's Fine. She's Not. 💛
ad_examples.body_excerpt:
She won't say anything. That's just who she is.
She'll wince getting up from the couch and tell you she's fine. She'll slow down on walks and say she's just tired. She's spent a lifetime taking care of everyone else, and asking for help isn't something that comes naturally to her.

But you see it. You notice the way she grips the armrest when she stands. The way mornings are harder than they used to be.

ad_examples.headline: Why Your Balance Got Worse Over Time
ad_examples.body_excerpt:
Here's something most people don't realize about balance and falls.

It's not just about being careful. Your feet have thousands of nerve endings that send constant signals to your brain, telling it exactly where the ground is and how to adjust your balance in real time.

Thick, cushioned soles muffle those signals. Raised heels tilt your body forward, throwing off your natural alignment. Your brain stops receiving the feedback it needs.

ad_examples.headline: Prevent Falls Or Don't Pay 💜
ad_examples.body_excerpt:
We're so confident these shoes will improve your balance that we'll let you test them for 30 days. If they don't work, you don't pay. Full refund. No questions.

Why are we that confident? Because we know what's causing your balance problems. And it's not age.
Thick, cushioned shoes block the signals your feet send to your brain to keep you steady. Your brain loses contact with the ground.

ad_examples.headline: We're That Confident. Try Us 👀
ad_examples.body_excerpt:
30 days. Wear them everywhere. If your neuropathy symptoms don't improve, send them back. Full refund. No questions asked.

We offer that because we've seen what happens when the compression finally stops.
Wide toe box. Flexible build. Flat stable sole.
Your feet decide. We're that confident.

36% OFF + Free Shipping + 30-Day Money-Back Guarantee.

ad_examples.headline: Why The Tingling Never Stops
ad_examples.body_excerpt:
The burning keeps coming back because the compression never stops.
Your shoe is squeezing the exact nerves causing it every single hour you wear it.

Wide toe box. Flexible build. Zero-drop sole. Hands-free entry.

Remove the compression. Give the nerves room. Watch what changes.
36% OFF + Free Shipping + 30-Day Money-Back Guarantee.

Don't become a statistic ❌

HF Stride barefoot shoes are designed to keep you steady.

ad_examples.headline: Only 100 Pairs Left
ad_examples.body_excerpt:
❤️ We're sorry! We didn't expect this.
After helping thousands of seniors feel steadier, more balanced, and more confident on their feet, our barefoot shoes sold out faster than expected.

We heard from so many people who told us they finally felt stable again while walking and we didn't want anyone else to miss out.

That's why we restocked just 100 pairs for a limited time.

ad_examples.headline: The Shoe That Prevents Falls
ad_examples.body_excerpt:
Balance issues and falls aren't inevitable. They're preventable. And it often comes down to one thing. What's on your feet.

Decades of thick cushioned shoes slowly disconnect your feet from the ground. Your foot muscles weaken. Your nerve endings go quiet. Your brain stops receiving the signals it needs to keep you balanced.

That's not aging. That's the wrong footwear.

HF Stride fixes that connection.

Beautiful enough to want. Functional enough to need. First run, limited pairs.

dominant_hooks: "I've never stepped into a shoe this easily—no bending, no fumbling. HF Stride's Hands-Off Entry is a game-changer!" – Maria, 58 (appears in ~8/30 recent ads — most repeated copy block in Dataset B)
"BOGO is back! Buy 1 pair and get 1 FREE. Hurry! It's for a limited time only." (6 near-identical BOGO ads running simultaneously — dominant summer promotional push)
"Does every step feel like walking on broken glass? Narrow, stiff shoes can squeeze sensitive toes and press against already-irritated feet." — rhetorical neuropathy question hook
"My feet used to feel like I was stepping on broken glass. HF Stride finally gave them room to move without constant pressure." — testimonial neuropathy variant

"Numbness should not control your entire life. It should not determine how far you walk, whether you cover your feet at night, or whether you can play with your grandkids." — life-scope limitation neuropathy hook (new emotional depth)
"Fall prevention you actually want to wear every single day. Not a clunky medical shoe. Not something that screams I have balance problems." — anti-stigma aesthetics hook (brand new angle)
"🚨 FINAL HOURS: SAVE 50% 🚨 Our top-selling barefoot shoe is flying off the shelves!" — urgency flash sale
"🇺🇸 HAPPY 4th OF JULY! 🇺🇸 Celebrate freedom with pain-free walking. For a limited time, get Buy 1, Get 1 FREE" — seasonal BOGO holiday tie-in
new_avatars: Style-aware senior woman who wants fall-prevention footwear that doesn't look medical — 'Restores Balance. Looks Great' ad explicitly addresses rejection of the 'clunky medical shoe' stigma; 'Google rated 4.8 stars by over 2 million women' targets fashion-aware female buyer

Diabetic neuropathy sufferer with life-scope limitations — 'Numbness should not control your entire life... whether you can play with your grandkids' elevates the avatar beyond foot pain to someone whose whole life participation is constrained by the condition
format_notes: Video proportion increased from 30% in proven winners (Dataset A) to 40% in recent ads — significant creative investment shift toward video
Image share more than doubled from 8% to 20% — testing static creative at higher volumes
DCO dropped from 62% to 40% — proven workhorse format being supplemented, not replaced
Carousel remains 0% across both datasets — HIKE has never tested carousel in active creative
Short-form video (15-20 sec) introduced in Dataset B alongside standard long-form (90-130 sec); testing format length split

New domain A/B test: www.michael-linda.com (attribution test for Linda 62 creative) and go.hike-footwear.com/pages/5-brands-comparison (new competitive comparison landing page — signals bottom-of-funnel brand comparison intent being targeted)

what_changed: BOGO mechanic completely absent in Dataset A top-50 (their proven winners never used BOGO), now the single most-deployed promotional frame in Dataset B with 6 identical variants — major summer strategy shift
Caregiver 'She Says She's Fine. She's Not.' angle (2 ads in Dataset A, both April 2026) has been fully pulled from recent creative — their most emotionally sophisticated ad series is on pause

Educational long-form mechanism ads ('Why Your Balance Got Worse Over Time', 'The Shoe That Prevents Falls' 300+ word copy, 'Why The Tingling Never Stops') are Dataset A staples but entirely absent from Dataset B
Clearance/hard-price creative ($33/pair, 99 Pairs Left) was a Feb 2026 one-off blitz and is fully gone from current creative
Seasonal holiday tie-in appeared for the first time in Dataset B (4th of July BOGO) — signals they are building a promotional calendar
New comparison landing page introduced (go.hike-footwear.com/pages/5-brands-comparison) — targeting bottom-of-funnel comparison shoppers, a new intent layer not present in Dataset A
new_angles: Anti-medical-shoe stigma: 'Fall prevention you actually want to wear every single day. Not a clunky medical shoe. Not something that screams I have balance problems.' — directly addresses the aesthetic objection that prevents seniors from buying functional shoes; brand new in Dataset B

Life-scope neuropathy stakes: 'Numbness should not control your entire life. It should not determine how far you walk, whether you cover your feet at night, or whether you can play with your grandkids.' — elevates consequence beyond foot comfort to life participation
Google rating social proof format: 'Google rated 4.8 stars by over 2 million women' — new proof type (rating-based) vs. Dataset A's volume-only proof ('1.4M–2M people')

Broken glass rhetorical question as cold-open hook: 'Does every step feel like walking on broken glass?' — in Dataset A this phrase only appeared buried inside testimonial body copy; now used as the opening hook
Competitor comparison landing page destination — new intent: capture bottom-of-funnel shoppers actively comparing HIKE vs. rivals before purchase
doubled_down: HF Stride Hands-Off Entry / step-in convenience: proportionally even more dominant in Dataset B (~8/30 ads vs. ~6/50 in Dataset A) — this is their confirmed hero feature angle heading into summer

Maria 58 and Linda 62 named testimonial quotes recycled verbatim across almost every HF Stride variant in Dataset B: 'These shoes feel like an extension of my feet, so light and supportive! – Linda, 62' and '"I've never stepped into a shoe this easily... game-changer!" – Maria, 58'
Urgency/scarcity language: 'FINAL HOURS', 'LAST REMAINING STOCK', 'flying off the shelves', 'BOGO for limited time only' — layered on every promotional ad
Free shipping + 30-day money-back guarantee as standard close — appears in every single ad across both datasets without exception
delta.strategic_note: HIKE is running a summer BOGO blitz while simultaneously testing a brand-new anti-stigma aesthetics angle ('Not something that screams I have balance problems') that they have not yet scaled — this gives OOBEND two immediate windows: (1) own the 'looks good AND heals feet' positioning before HIKE scales it, since OOBEND's KyotoStep knit design already has the aesthetic story to tell; (2) own the caregiver gift-buyer lane ('She Says She's Fine') which HIKE just vacated from active creative, running zero caregiver-targeted ads right now despite it being one of their proven Dataset A angles.

dominant_hooks: "Long Shifts Are Tough - Your Shoes Shouldn't Make Them Harder" (nurse/shift-worker opener — oldest ads in set, running since Dec 2025, highest cumulative impressions)
"Our End Of Season Sale Offer Is Here With A Buy 1 Get 1 Offer On Barefoot Shoes... Get It While You Can" (urgency BOGO — appears across 9+ ads)
"Snore Less & Sleep Better To Wake Up Refreshed / Because Buy 1 Get 1 Free Sale Is ON" (snoring + BOGO combo — 5 ads)
"Neck Ache, Dead Arms & Flat Pillows Ruining Your Sleep?" (stacked symptom opener — 3 ads)
"😫 Waking Up With Constant Neck Aches?" / "Sleeping 7-8 Hours But Waking Up Rubbing Your Neck?" (sleep-pain cause hook — 3 ads)
"Looking For Easy-Wear Shoes That Don't Make Walking Feel Like A Chore?" (mobility softener — 2 ads)
"💤 Your Pillow Could Be The Hidden Reason You Wake Up Stiff" (hidden-cause blame-shift — 1 ad, high-performing angle)
"🦶 Elderly With Foot Discomfort?" / "😓 Tired Of Foot Aches & Discomfort?" (explicit senior pain direct address — 4 ads)
"Stacking, Folding & Re-Adjusting Pillows All Night Isn't Comfort" (behavioral pain mirror — 1 ad)
"Modern Shoes With Thick Cushioned Soles Block Your Connection From Earth's Energy" (grounding/earthing angle — 1 ad)
avatar_profiles: Female nurse or healthcare worker aged 30-50, on 12-hour standing shifts, feet swell by end of day, needs roomy breathable footwear that stays light the entire shift
Senior aged 55-75 with chronic foot discomfort (aching, swelling, restricted toes), looking for easy slip-on comfort shoes that don't require bending down or dealing with laces
Middle-aged to older adult (45-70) who wakes up with neck aches or stiff shoulders, suspects their pillow is the culprit but hasn't changed it yet
Couple sharing a bed where one partner snores — buyer is the lighter sleeper seeking dual-benefit product (BOGO framing capitalises on buying for partner)
Barefoot/natural movement enthusiast interested in zero-drop sole, grounding benefits, and foot strengthening — somewhat health-ideological buyer
pain_points: Foot aches and discomfort generally (appears across ~18 of 50 ads — highest frequency)
Squished, cramped, or restricted toes from conventional shoes (~12 ads)
Neck aches and stiffness upon waking (~12 ads)
Restless sleep, tossing and turning, unable to stay asleep (~10 ads)
BOGO/value-seeking — paying full price feels wrong when comfort is lacking (~9 ads with BOGO framing)
Heavy or dead arms after sleeping (~5 ads)
Snoring — disrupted breathing at night affecting sleep quality (~5 ads)
Tired, heavy feet after long work shifts (~5 ads)
Morning shoulder stiffness and pressure (~5 ads)
Pillows that flatten, shift, or fail to maintain shape overnight (~5 ads)
offer_mechanics: BOGO (Buy 1 Get 1 Free) — dominant mechanic used across both shoes and pillows; approximately 18 of 50 ads lead with or feature BOGO prominently
30-Day / 30-Night Money-Back Guarantee — present in effectively every single ad, positioned as the risk-removal close
"End of Season Sale" branded BOGO — recurring seasonal framing layered on top of BOGO to add urgency (9 ads use this exact phrase)
"Boxing Day" BOGO variant — same BOGO mechanic with seasonal label
Two pillows for $119.99 — explicit price anchor on the bundle mentioned in one pillow ad
Free shipping Australia-wide — mentioned in select ads as a sweetener
Limited time urgency cues — "Won't Stick Around", "While You Can", "This BOGO Deal Won't Last"

dominant_hooks: "Neck Discomfort, Shoulder Pressure, Night Sweats & Restlessness? 😣 / You May Not Have A Sleep Problem… Your Pillow Might Be The Reason" — new causal-reframe hook, appears in 4+ ads; adds night sweats as a pain point not seen in Dataset A

"Spending Money On Useless Stuff But Not On Better Sleep? / Buy 2 Butterfly Pillow & Get 5 Freebies" — upgraded bundle mechanic (vs simple BOGO in A); freebies include sleep mask, earplugs, laundry bag, pillowcase, and bonus sleep accessory, appears in 5+ ads

"You Spend Nearly One-Third Of Your Life On Your Pillow / So Why Are So Many People Still Sleeping On The Same Old Flat Pillow They've Had For Years?" — time/investment hook reframing the pillow as a 33% of life decision; 3 ads
"Our Comfiest Barefoot Shoes Are Down To Just $43 Per Pair / That's Any Style. Any Color" — hard price-drop anchor replacing BOGO for shoes, 6 ads
"A Bad Night Of Sleep Doesn't Just Affect Your Night... It Follows You Into The Next Day / Feeling Tired, Foggy, Irritable Or Constantly Running On Empty" — ripple-effect hook on sleep quality, 1 ad
"All Butterfly Pillows Are Not Created Equal - Some Skip The Memory Foam & Don't Provide Head Support" — category-differentiation / authenticity challenge hook, 2 ads
"Most People Don't Realise How Restricted Their Feet Feel... Until They Try Shoes That Actually Let Their Toes Move Naturally" — awareness-gap opener for shoes (no seniors framing), 2 ads
new_avatars: Universal adult who doesn't sleep well but hasn't identified their pillow as the cause — broader than prior neck-pain sufferer; night sweats included explicitly, suggesting possible menopausal/hormonal overlap
General adult barefoot/comfort seeker motivated by value ("$43 per pair, any style, any color") rather than senior identity or foot pathology — less medicalised, more lifestyle
Authenticity-aware buyer skeptical of imitation products — targeted by "All Butterfly Pillows Are Not Created Equal" angle, implies prior buyer awareness of category
format_notes: Video remains the dominant format at approximately 47% of Dataset B (30 ads: 14 video, 15 image, 0 carousel, 1 mixed) — slight shift toward more image testing vs Dataset A (52% video in A)
Carousel format has completely disappeared in the newest 30 ads (was 2% in A with the 'Stop Using The Wrong Pillow' ad from March 2026)
No DCO/dynamic formats in either dataset — PFA is running static creative tests at scale
All 30 recent ads are vertical 9:16 videos or square 1:1 images — consistent with Meta feed/Reels placements; no feed banner or horizontal formats detected

what_changed: Nurse/shift-worker shoe angle completely abandoned in Dataset B — the two oldest and highest-impression ads in the entire account (running since Dec 2025) targeted nurses explicitly; zero nurse mentions in the newest 30 ads
Barefoot shoe offer mechanic shifted from BOGO (Buy 1 Get 1 Free) in A to flat price-drop anchor ($43/pair, any style, any color) in B — signals BOGO may be fatiguing for the shoe SKU
Pillow offer upgraded from simple BOGO to 'Buy 2 Get 5 Freebies' bundle in B — adds perceived value without another pair-for-free mechanic, likely AOV optimization
Carousel format disappeared entirely in recent 30 ads (was used once in A for 'Stop Using The Wrong Pillow' educational content)

Senior-explicit framing in shoe ads softened in B — Dataset A had direct 'Barefoot Shoes For Seniors' and 'Elderly With Foot Discomfort' headlines; B broadens to 'Suitable For Seniors Or Anyone Experiencing Restricted Or Tired Feet' or drops senior mention entirely
Stretch Comfort Shoes (women's slip-on) product has zero ads in Dataset B — all recent shoe activity is barefoot only
new_angles: Causal-reframe pillow hook: 'You May Not Have A Sleep Problem… Your Pillow Might Be The Reason' — shifts blame from the person to the product, reducing resistance; night sweats added as a new pain point not present in Dataset A
Time-investment hook: 'You Spend Nearly One-Third Of Your Life On Your Pillow / So Why Are So Many People Still Sleeping On The Same Old Flat Pillow' — reframes $X spend as a ratio-of-life decision

Ripple-effect hook: 'A Bad Night Of Sleep Doesn't Just Affect Your Night... It Follows You Into The Next Day / Feeling Tired, Foggy, Irritable Or Constantly Running On Empty' — connects sleep quality to daytime performance consequences
Category-authenticity challenge: 'All Butterfly Pillows Are Not Created Equal' — pre-empts commodity comparison shopping and positions PFA as the genuine article vs knockoffs

Liberation angle for shoes: 'Most People Don't Realise How Restricted Their Feet Feel... Until They Try Shoes That Actually Let Their Toes Move Naturally' — awareness-gap hook that doesn't require buyer to already identify as having foot pain
Hard price-drop scarcity: 'Down To Just $43 Per Pair. This Is Your Last Chance Before They're Gone Forever' — combined price anchor + FOMO replacing the BOGO mechanic for shoes
doubled_down: 'Your Pillow Is Fake' / 'Your Pillow Could Be The Hidden Reason You Wake Up Stiff' — appeared in Dataset A (April 2026 ads) and is being continued into June 2026 with a near-identical hook, suggesting strong CTR signal
30-Day / 30-Night Money-Back Guarantee — present in 100% of both datasets; it is now a table-stakes trust floor, not a differentiator
Video as primary format — stays at roughly 47-52% across both datasets; PFA is not abandoning video despite heavy image testing
Pillow as primary SKU — Dataset A was 48% pillow / 52% shoe by ad count; Dataset B is approximately 67% pillow / 33% shoe, showing resource shift toward the pillow category
delta.strategic_note: PFA is actively de-emphasising shoe advertising (abandoning their highest-impression nurse angle, reducing senior-explicit copy, pausing Stretch Comfort Shoes entirely) in favour of scaling their Butterfly Pillow — this opens a direct window for OOBEND to own the 'medical-adjacent foot pain + everyday mobility' shoe niche in the US without fighting PFA's peak creative energy. Additionally, PFA's most effective new hook framework ('You May Not Have A [problem type]… Your [product] Might Be The Reason') is directly adaptable for OOBEND: e.g. 'You May Not Have A Balance Problem… Your Shoes Might Be The Reason' or 'You May Not Have Neuropathy Pain All Day… Your Footwear Might Be Making It Worse' — this causal-reframe pattern combined with OOBEND's HSA/FSA eligibility and medical positioning could be a high-conviction test for Q3 2026.

dominant_hooks: Testimonial opener (100% of ads): "I thought my feet would never feel normal again - until I tried these."
Problem validation (100%): "That's the kind of thing we hear every day from people dealing with nerve pain and foot numbness."

Science credibility bridge (100%): "The copper grounding plug in the sole helps reconnect your body to the Earth's electrons, which studies show can reduce inflammation, improve nerve conductivity, and even ease neuropathic symptoms."
Last-resort qualifier (100%): "They're lightweight, barefoot-style, and made for people who've already tried everything else."
Symptom targeting (100%): "If your feet feel like pins and needles... if you struggle with balance or can't feel the floor beneath you - this could change everything."
Risk-reversal CTA (100%): "Try them with 50% off and a 90-day money-back guarantee."
avatar_profiles: Neuropathy sufferer 55-75, experiencing persistent pins-and-needles, numbness, and loss of sensation in feet — has cycled through orthotics, insoles, compression socks, and standard comfort shoes with no lasting relief
Diabetic foot patient dealing with neuropathic symptoms (inflammation, reduced nerve conductivity, proprioception loss) and looking for a non-pharmaceutical adjunct solution
Senior 65+ with balance/stability problems caused by inability to feel floor — fear of falling, cautious about activity level

Desperate last-resort buyer who self-identifies as having 'tried everything else' — high intent, skeptical but pain-motivated, drawn to pseudo-scientific 'earthing' narrative as explanation for why nothing else worked
pain_points: Nerve pain / neuropathy (100% of ads — primary focus of entire creative)
Loss of proprioception — can't feel floor beneath feet (100%)
Systemic inflammation (100% — addressed via 'earthing' science claim)
Feeling like feet will 'never feel normal again' — hopelessness/resignation (100%)
offer_mechanics: 50% off (stated explicitly in every ad body)
90-day money-back guarantee (every ad — strong risk reversal)
BOGO volume deal — primary landing page is /collections/barefoot-shoes-bogo-volume (all 50 Dataset A ads point here)
Secondary landing: direct product pages for Bronoir Skin Pro and Bronoir Flex models (seen in Dataset B variants)
'Try them' framing — positions purchase as low-risk experiment rather than commitment
No urgency/scarcity claims — offer appears evergreen

ad_examples.headline: STOP WALKING ON PAIN - TRY THIS 👟
ad_examples.body_excerpt: "I thought my feet would never feel normal again - until I tried these." That's the kind of thing we hear every day from people dealing with nerve pain and foot numbness. Bronoir Grounding Shoes aren't magic - but they're designed around real science: The copper grounding plug in the sole helps reconnect your body to the Earth's electrons, which studies show can reduce inflammation, improve nerve conductivity, and even ease neuropathic symptoms. They're lightweight, barefoot-style, and made for people who've already tried everything else. 👉 Try them with 50% off and a 90-day money-back guarantee.
